---
title: ETC4500/ETC5450 Advanced&nbsp;R&nbsp;programming
author: "Week 4: R programming with LLMs"
format:
  presentation-beamer:
    fontsize: "14pt,t"
    section-titles: false
    toc: true
    knitr:
      opts_chunk:
        dev: "cairo_pdf"
    fig-width: 7.5
    fig-height: 3.5
    include-in-header: ../header.tex
    keep-tex: true
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
#| cache: false
source(here::here("setup.R"))
source(here::here("course_info.R"))
```


# Terminology

## Terminology
\fontsize{14}{16}\sf\vspace*{-0.3cm}

  * **Provider**: Service that hosts LLM and provides API for interacting with it (e.g., Anthropic)
  * **Model**: Specific version of LLM (e.g., Claude-Sonnet-4.6)
  * **Prompt**: Input you give LLM to generate a response.
  * **System prompt**: Special type of prompt that sets behaviour of LLM.
  * **Tokens**: Units of text that the LLM processes (words, parts of words, punctuation, etc.).
  * **Context**: Information available to LLM when generating a response
  * **Tool**: Function or API that LLM can call to perform specific tasks

# Positron Assistant

## Set up Positron

### GitHub Copilot

* Make sure you are logged into your GitHub account.
* GitHub Copilot settings: <https://github.com/settings/copilot/features>

### Anthropic Claude

  * Set up Claude as a model provider using an Anthropic API key: <https://platform.claude.com/>
* `Ctrl-Shift-P`: "Positron Assistant: Configure Language Model Providers"


## Code completion

GitHub Copilot provides code completion suggestions as you type.

You can accept suggestions by pressing `Tab` or `Enter`

Snooze it by clicking the icon at bottom right of the editor, or by pressing `Ctrl+Shift+P` and selecting "GitHub Copilot: Toggle"

Try typing a function name or a comment describing what you want to do, and see what suggestions Copilot gives you.

## Default chat agents

* **Ask**: Provides answers and code suggestions without automatic execution
* **Edit**: Suggests code changes for you to apply to files
* **Agent**: Autonomously executes work including running code and modifying files
* **Plan**: Creates detailed step-by-step plans before execution

You will need a model. Default is GPT-5 mini (slower and less capable than a paid model).

## Pick model

* GitHub Copilot: Code completion and chat agents

* Claude: Chat agents with more detailed reasoning and planning capabilities

## Chat agent: Ask

* How do I plot a histogram of the `value` variable in my dataset `my_data`?
* Why am I getting a syntax error in my R script?
* Can you explain what this function does?

## Attach context

* Default context: active editor file, and active interpreter session.
* Add any other context you think will help.
* Interpreter session context includes code inputs and outputs in that session, in-memory objects in the Variables pane, the active plot in the Plots pane.

## Chat agent: Edit

* Refactor this function to be more efficient.
* Add error handling to this code block.
* Update this script to use the latest version of the `dplyr` package.

## Chat agent: Agent

* Create a new R script that loads my dataset, performs some basic data cleaning, and generates a summary report.
* Install the required packages and run this analysis script for me.
* Update all my ggplot graphics to use a color-blind friendly palette.

## Chat agent: Plan

* Outline the steps needed to turn this script into an R package.
* Plan how to add tests for this R script
* Create a step-by-step plan to optimize this data processing pipeline.

## Git help

* You can ask the agent to generate commit messages for you, or even create commits and push them to GitHub.

* You can ask the agent to review your code and suggest improvements, or even automatically apply those improvements.

## Slash commands

* `/fix` - Fixes code
* `/explain` - Explains code
* `/exportQuarto` - Generates a Quarto document from the current chat conversation

## Prompting tips

* Be specific about what you want the agent to do.
* Provide relevant context to help the agent understand your request.
* Review the agent's suggestions carefully before accepting them, especially if they involve code changes or execution.
* Use the planning agent for complex tasks that require multiple steps or reasoning.

## Exercise

* Ask the `Edit` agent to update the `fizzbuzz()` function to allow numbers other than 5 and 7 to be used.
* Use the `Edit` agent to produce a roxygen header and unit tests for the `fizzbuzz()` function we wrote in week 2 (<http://arp.numbat.space/week4/fizzbuzz.R>).
* Ask the `Plan` agent to outline the steps needed to turn the `fizzbuzz()` function into an R package, including adding documentation and tests.

# Other tools

## Other tools

* Databot: a Positron extension that provides data analysis capabilities using LLMs, based on the same set up as Positron Assistant.
* ellmer package: A package to interact with LLMs from R, including prompt templates and agent frameworks.
* shinychat: Build your own custom chat interface using Shiny, and connect it to an LLM using the ellmer package.

## Databot

* Interactive data analysis tool that allows you to ask questions about your data and get answers in the form of code, visualizations, and explanations.
* Uses LLMs to generate code and explanations based on your questions.
* Can write Quarto reports based on your data and analysis.

## ellmer package

* Allows you to interact with LLMs programmatically from R.
* Beware of long conversations. The entire conversation history is sent with each request.
* Allows a system prompt to set the behaviour of the agent.
* Allows tools to be registered.
* Provides a framework for building custom agents
